control_title: Maintain current contact details
control_description: Ensure that AWS account contact information is current and up to date.
reason: Root account contact details have not been reviewed in the past 6 months.

control_title: Ensure no root account access key exists
control_description: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. AWS Access Keys provide programmatic access to a given AWS account. It is recommended that all access keys associated with the root account be removed.
reason: Root account has active access keys (KeyId: AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE).

control_title: Ensure MFA is enabled for the root account
control_description: The root account is the most privileged user in an AWS account. Multi-factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection on top of a user name and password.
reason: Root account does not have MFA enabled.

control_title: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater
control_description: Password policies are used to enforce password complexity requirements. IAM password policies can be used to ensure that passwords are at least a given length.
reason: Minimum password length is currently 8; required minimum is 14.

control_title: Ensure IAM password policy prevents password reuse
control_description: IAM password policies can prevent the reuse of a given password by the same user. It is recommended that the password policy prevent the reuse of passwords.
reason: Password reuse prevention is not enabled.

control_title: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for all IAM users that have a console password
control_description: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password.
reason: IAM user alice has console password enabled but MFA not configured.

control_title: Ensure multi-factor authentication (MFA) is enabled for all IAM users that have a console password
control_description: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password.
reason: IAM user bob has console password enabled but MFA not configured.

control_title: Ensure a support role has been created to manage incidents with AWS Support
control_description: AWS provides a support centre that can be used for incident notification and response as well as technical support and customer services.
reason: No IAM role or user with AWSSupportAccess policy found.

control_title: Ensure S3 Bucket Policy is set to deny HTTP requests
control_description: At the Amazon S3 bucket level a bucket policy can be set to deny insecure connections. It is recommended to set this policy.
reason: S3 bucket my-public-bucket does not have a bucket policy denying HTTP requests.

control_title: Ensure S3 Bucket Policy is set to deny HTTP requests
control_description: At the Amazon S3 bucket level a bucket policy can be set to deny insecure connections. It is recommended to set this policy.
reason: S3 bucket my-data-bucket does not have a bucket policy denying HTTP requests.

control_title: Ensure MFA Delete is enabled on S3 buckets
control_description: Once MFA Delete is enabled on your sensitive and classified S3 bucket it requires the user to have two forms of authentication.
reason: S3 bucket my-data-bucket does not have MFA Delete enabled.

control_title: Ensure EBS volume encryption is enabled in all regions
control_description: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) supports encryption at rest when using the Elastic Block Store (EBS) service.
reason: EBS volume vol-0123456789abcdef0 is not encrypted.

control_title: Ensure that encryption-at-rest is enabled for RDS Instances
control_description: Amazon RDS encrypted DB instances use industry standard AES-256 encryption algorithm to encrypt your data on the server that hosts your Amazon RDS DB instances.
reason: RDS instance db-prod01 does not have storage encryption enabled.

control_title: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
control_description: AWS CloudTrail is a web service that records AWS API calls for your account and delivers log files to you.
reason: CloudTrail trail prod-trail is not a multi-region trail.

control_title: Ensure CloudTrail log file validation is enabled
control_description: CloudTrail log file validation creates a digitally signed digest file containing a hash of each log that CloudTrail writes to S3.
reason: CloudTrail trail prod-trail does not have log file validation enabled.

control_title: Ensure the S3 bucket used to store CloudTrail logs is not publicly accessible
control_description: CloudTrail logs a record of every API call made in your AWS account.
reason: S3 bucket cloudtrail-logs-123456789012 has public read ACL enabled.

control_title: Ensure CloudTrail logs are encrypted at rest using KMS CMKs
control_description: AWS CloudTrail is a web service that records AWS API calls for an account and makes those logs available to users and resources in accordance with IAM policies.
reason: CloudTrail trail prod-trail is not encrypted with a KMS CMK.

control_title: Ensure a log metric filter and alarm exist for unauthorized API calls
control_description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms.
reason: No metric filter for unauthorized API calls found in CloudWatch log groups.

control_title: Ensure a log metric filter and alarm exist for Management Console sign-in without MFA
control_description: You can do real-time monitoring of API calls by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms.
reason: No metric filter for console sign-in without MFA found.

control_title: Ensure a log metric filter and alarm exist for usage of root account
control_description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms.
reason: No metric filter and alarm for root account usage found.

control_title: Ensure no Network ACLs allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
control_description: The Network Access Control List (NACL) function as a stateless packet filter to control ingress and egress traffic for subnets within a VPC.
reason: Network ACL acl-12345678 allows unrestricted inbound TCP access on port 22 (SSH).

control_title: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to remote server administration ports
control_description: Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress and egress network traffic to AWS resources.
reason: Security group sg-12345 allows unrestricted inbound access on ports 22 and 3389.

control_title: Ensure the default security group of every VPC restricts all traffic
control_description: A VPC comes with a default security group whose initial settings deny all inbound traffic and allow all outbound traffic.
reason: Default security group sg-default in VPC vpc-12345678 allows inbound traffic.
